name: Safe synthetic playbook: Communication with categorized threat intel indicator
description: Netflow heuristic tied to an abstract threat-intelligence category in the lab (no routable IPs, no C2 configs). Represents how SOC would ingest a benign sinkhole-equivalent classification for testing correlation only.

simulated_timeline.synthetic_narrative: Lab intel bundle adds category label LAB-IOC-SINKHOLE-STUB without embedding addresses.
Netflow summary fixture notes outbound session toward an indicator bucket tagged malicious_category_synthetic.
IR opens lightweight task to inventory affected internal host list (labels only).

payload.title: LAB: Session matched synthetic malicious intel bucket (no live C2)

events_emitted.headline: Intel category published to lab aggregator
events_emitted.synthetic_detail: Demonstrates ingestion path without leaking real subscriber indicators.

expected_agents_triggered.triggered_reason_synthetic: Threat intel alignment is primary driver.
Potential C2 egress triggers IR monitoring lane.

expected_remediation.summary: Block indicator category at perimeter simulation, sweep affected hosts for persistence checklist (generic), escalate if volume grows.

expected_autonomous_remediation.synthetic_action: Attach latest intel bulletin stub to incident record.

expected_human_approval_gates.synthetic_rationale: Avoid blocking legitimate CDN edges mis-tagged in lab bundles.

expected_final_report.title: Malicious intel category correlation — synthetic playbook outcome
expected_final_report.synthetic_executive_summary: Lab feeds produced a benign sinkhole-category match; playbook validates correlation and optional blocking without transmitting real IOC material.
